description: Track and broadcast user presence status (online, away, busy, offline) with automatic detection based on connection and activity

actors.name: User
actors.description: The user whose presence is being tracked

actors.name: Presence Service
actors.description: Server-side service that manages connection tracking and status aggregation

actors.name: Status Subscriber
actors.description: Any user or system observing another user's presence

values.label: Online
values.description: User is actively connected and available

values.label: Busy
values.description: User is connected but does not wish to be disturbed

transitions.description: User connection becomes idle past the away threshold
User manually sets status to busy
User activity detected on an idle connection
All user connections are closed or lost
All connections lost while user was away
All connections lost while user was busy
User manually sets status to online
User manually sets status to away
User manually sets status to busy

general: Each user connection is tracked independently; overall presence is derived from all active connections
When a user's last connection is removed, their presence transitions to offline automatically
Users may manually override auto-detected presence with online, away, or busy
Status text is user-controlled and persists independently of the connection state
An invisible or disabled status may be allowed or blocked by system configuration
Rate limiting must be applied to manual status changes to prevent abuse
Presence updates must be broadcast to all subscribers in real time
Stale connections on a node must be cleaned up when that node goes offline
Custom status names can be defined system-wide; each maps to one of the base status types
Status text changes require that the system allows user status message modifications

given: user authenticates and opens a connection
connection_established.result: Connection is registered and user status is set to online

given: user submits a status change request
status_set_manually.result: User status is updated and broadcast to all subscribers

given: user submits a status text change
status_text_updated.result: Status message is persisted and broadcast

given: a user connection is closed or timed out
connection_removed.result: Connection removed; if no connections remain, user transitions to offline

given: last active connection for the user is closed
last_connection_removed.result: User status changes to offline and subscribers are notified

invisible_status_blocked.result: Status change rejected because invisible mode is disabled by system configuration

given: user exceeds allowed rate of manual status changes
status_change_rate_limited.result: Request rejected due to rate limiting; user must wait before changing status again

given: user attempts to change status text
status_text_change_not_allowed.result: Status text change rejected because the feature is disabled by system configuration

errors.message: Invisible status is not available on this server
Too many status changes. Please wait before trying again
Status message changes are not allowed on this server

events.name: presence.status_changed
events.description: Fired when a user's presence status or status text changes

events.name: presence.connection_added
events.description: Fired when a new user connection is registered

events.name: presence.connection_removed
events.description: Fired when a user connection is closed or removed

related.reason: User identity is required to associate presence with an account
Typing indicators complement presence by showing in-room activity
Read receipts may use last-seen timestamps that relate to presence

goals.description: Maintain and broadcast accurate user presence status in real time, reflecting connection and activity changes promptly

success_metrics.measurement: Maximum lag between connection event and status broadcast to subscribers
Stale connections cleared when node goes offline / total stale connections

invariants: when the last connection is removed the user transitions to offline
manual status overrides are preserved until explicitly changed or connection is lost
rate limiting prevents abuse of manual status changes

acceptance_tests.expect: status transitions to offline and subscribers are notified

capabilities.description: Derive user presence from active connection records and broadcast changes in real time
Allow users to manually set their status subject to system-level policy

tradeoffs.reason: presence updates are best-effort broadcasts; brief staleness is acceptable over blocking message delivery
